title: Wood: a valuable resource in New Zealand's economy
originalFilename: 7360_Passage 1 Wood a valuable resource in New Zealand's economy.html

passages.title: Wood: a valuable resource in New Zealand's economy
passages.source_html:
Wood: a valuable resource in New Zealand's economy


A


During the settlement of New Zealand by European immigrants, natural timbers played a major role. Wood was easily accessible and relatively cheap. A tradition of wooden houses arose, supported by the recognition that they were less likely to collapse suddenly during earthquakes, a not infrequent event in this part of the world. But in addition to demand from the domestic no market, there was also a demand for forest products from overseas.


B


Early explorers recognised the suitability of the tall, straight trunks of the kauri for constructing sailing vessels. The kauri is a species of coniferous tree found only in small areas of the southern hemisphere. So from the early 1800s, huge amounts of this type of wood were sold to Australia and the UK for that purpose. For a period, the forestry industry was the country's major export earner, but the rate of harvest was unsustainable and, by the beginning of the 20th century, indigenous timber exports were rapidly declining.


C


From the 1940s, newly established plantations of an imported species of tree called radiata pine supplied timber and other wood products in increasing quantities. By the 1960s, plantation-grown timber was providing most of the country's sawn timber needs, especially for construction. Today, less than two percent of timber is cut from indigenous forests, and almost all of that is used for higher-value end uses, such as furniture and fittings.


D


As the pine industry developed, it became apparent that this type of wood was also well suited for many uses. It makes excellent pulp* and is frequently used for posts, poles, furnishings and mouldings, particleboard, fibreboard, and for plywood and 'engineered' wood products. Pine by-products are used in the chemical and pharmaceutical industries and residues are consumed for fuel. This amazing versatility has encouraged the development of an integrated forest-products industry which is almost unique in the world.


E


Exporters of wood products have largely targeted the rapidly growing markets of South and East Asia and Australia 80 percent of exports by value go to only five markets: Japan, Korea, China, the United States and Australia. The product mix remains heavily based on raw materials, with logs, sawn wood, pulp and paper comprising 75 percent of export value. However, finished wood products such as panels and furniture components are exported to more than 50 countries.


F 


In New Zealand itself, the construction industry is the principal user of solid wood products, servicing around 20,000 new house starts annually. However, the small size of New Zealand's population (just over four million) plus its small manufacturing and remanufacturing base, limit the forestry industry's domestic opportunities. For the last few years local wood consumption has been around only four million cubic metres. Accordingly, the development of the export market is the key to the industry's growth and contribution to the national economy in decades to come.


G


In 2004, forestry export receipts were about 11 percent of the country's total export income, their value having increased steadily for ten years, until affected by the exchange fluctuations and shipping costs of recent years. The forestry industry is New Zealand's third largest export sector, generating around $3,3 billion annually from logs and processed wood products. But it is generally agreed that it is operating well below its capacity and, with the domestic market already at its peak, almost all of the extra wood produced in future will have to be marketed overseas. That presents a major marketing challenge for the industry.


H


Although the export of logs will continue to provide valuable earnings for forest owners, there is broad acceptance that the industry must be based on value-added products in future. So the industry is investigating various processing, infrastructure and investment strategies with a view to increasing the level of local manufacturing before export. The keys to future success will depend on a variety of factor, better international marketing, product innovation, internationally competitive processing, better infrastructure and a suitable political, regulatory and investment environment. The industry claims that, given the right conditions, by 2025 the forestry sector could be the country's biggest export earner, generating $20 billion a year and employing 60,000 people.


I


One competitive advantage that New Zealand has is its ability to source large quantities of softwood from renewable forests. Consumers in several key wood markets are becoming more worried about sustainability, and the industry is supporting the development of national standards as well as the recognition of these internationally. However, New Zealand is not the only country with a plantation-style forestry industry. Chitzx, Brazil, Argentina, South Africa and Australia all have extensive plantings of fast-growing species (hardwood and softwood) and in the northern hemisphere, Scandinavian countries have all expanded their forests or controlled their use in the interests of future production.


J


Finally, in addition to competition from other wood producers, New Zealand faces competition from goods such as wood substitutes. These include steel framing for houses. This further underlines the necessity for globally competitive production and marketing strategies.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 6


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

Settlers realised that wooden houses were more dangerous than other types of structure.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位词: settlers, wooden houses, more dangerous


对应原文: A段，Wood was easily accessible and relatively cheap. A tradition of wooden houses arose, supported by the recognition that they were less likely to collapse suddenly during earthquakes, a not infrequent event in this part of the world.


详细解析: A段指出木屋在地震中更不容易倒塌（less likely to collapse），表明木屋比其他结构更安全，而非更危险。题目说木屋“更危险”，与原文含义相反，其中“more dangerous” 与“less likely to collapse”相互矛盾。

2

During the 1800s, New Zealand exported wood for use in boat-building.


正确答案： A. TRUE

定位词: 1800s, New Zealand, boat-building


对应原文: B段, Early explorers recognised the suitability of the tall, straight trunks of the kauri for constructing sailing vessels. The kauri is a species of coniferous tree found only in small areas of the southern hemisphere. So from the early 1800s, huge amounts of this type of wood were sold to Australia and the UK for that purpose. 


详细解析: B段指出，从19世纪早期起，大量木材被出售至澳大利亚和英国用于造船（that purpose），与题目表述一致，说明新西兰确实在1800年代出口木材用于建造船只。


同义替换：boat-building = constructing sailing vessels

3

Plantation-grown wood is generally better for construction than native-forest wood.


正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位词: plantation-grown wood, better for construction, native-forest wood


对应原文: C段，By the 1960s, plantation-grown timber was providing most of the country's sawn timber needs, especially for construction.


详细解析: C段指出种植林木材逐渐满足了新西兰国内建筑需求，但并未提及其优于或劣于本地森林木材，文中没有关于两者在建筑用途上好坏的直接对比信息。

4

Compared to other types of wood, pine has a narrow range of uses.


正确答案： B. FALSE

定位词: pine, narrow range of uses


对应原文: D段, This amazing versatility has encouraged the development of an integrated forest-products industry which is almost unique in the world.


详细解析: D段指出了松木的多种用途，例如用作纸浆、柱子、家具、模具等，并且称其用途非常广泛（amazing versatility），与题目中“用途有限”形成反差。其中"narrow range of uses” 与 “amazing versatility” 相矛盾。

5

Demand for housing in New Zealand is predicted to fall in the next few years.


正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位词: Demand for housing, New Zealand, fall,  in the next few years


对应原文: F段，In New Zealand itself, the construction industry is the principal user of solid wood products, servicing around 20,000 new house starts annually.


详细解析: F段提到新西兰每年有大约20,000新房开工，但未提及未来几年是否会出现需求下降的预测。


6

In future, the expansion of New Zealand’s wood industry will depend on its exports.


正确答案： A. TRUE

定位词: in the future, the expansion of New Zealand’s wood industry


对应原文:F段, Accordingly, the development of the export market is the key to the industry's growth and contribution to the national economy in decades to come.


详细解析: F段提到，未来木材行业的扩展依赖于出口市场的发展（the development of the export market is the key），说明行业的未来增长将依靠出口市场来实现，其中"depend on its exports” =“the development of the export market is the key”，表明出口市场是未来行业增长的关键。


同义替换：the expansion of New Zealand’s wood industry = the industry's growth; In future = In decades to come


Questions 7 - 13


Complete the questions. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

7

Apart from exchange rates, which factor has had a negative impact on New Zealand's forestry exports?

正确答案： 7 shipping costs
Question 7

定位词: exchange rates, negative impact, New Zealand's forestry exports


对应原文: G段, "In 2004, forestry export receipts were about 11 percent of the country's total export income, their value having increased steadily for ten years, until affected by the exchange fluctuations and shipping costs of recent years."


详细解析: G段指出，除了汇率波动（exchange fluctuations），运费（shipping costs）也对出口产生了负面影响。


同义替换：exchange rates = exchange fluctuations

8

Which part of New Zealand's economy does the forestry industry rank third in? 

正确答案： 8 export sector
Question 8

定位词: New Zealand's economy, forestry industry, rank third

 

对应原文: G段, The forestry industry is New Zealand's third largest export sector, generating around $3,3 billion annually from logs and processed wood products. 

 

详细解析: 原文直接提到林业是新西兰第三大出口行业（third largest export sector）。


同义替换：ranks third = third largest

9

According to the New Zealand forestry industry, what could be the size of its workforce by 2025? 

正确答案： 9 60,000##60000##60,000 people##60000 people
Question 9

定位词: New Zealand forestry industry, size ,workforce, 2025

 

对应原文: H段, The industry claims that, given the right conditions, by 2025 the forestry sector could be the country's biggest export earner, generating $20 billion a year and employing 60,000 people.

 

详细解析: 原文提到预计到2025年，林业将雇用60,000人。


同义替换：size of its workforce = employing 60,000 people


10

What kind of timber product is available in large amounts from renewable forests in New Zealand? 

正确答案： 10 softwood
Question 10

定位词: timber product, large amounts, renewable forests

 

对应原文: I段, One competitive advantage that New Zealand has is its ability to source large quantities of softwood from renewable forests.

 

详细解析: 原文指出新西兰从可再生林中获得大量“软木”（softwood）。


同义替换：large amounts = large quantities

11

Which aspect of timber production are New Zealand's main customers increasingly concerned about? 

正确答案： 11 sustainability
Question 11

定位词: timber production, customers, increasingly concerned about

 

对应原文: I段, Consumers in several key wood markets are becoming more worried about sustainability, and the industry is supporting the development of national standards as well as the recognition of these internationally. 

 

详细解析: 原文指出关键市场的消费者对可持续性（sustainability）问题越来越关注。


同义替换：Increasingly concerned about = becoming more worried about

12

Outside the southern hemisphere, who are New Zealand forestry's main competitors? 

正确答案： 12 Scandinavian countries
Question 12

定位词: southern hemisphere, competitors

 

对应原文: I段, However, New Zealand is not the only country with a plantation-style forestry industry. Chitzx, Brazil, Argentina, South Africa and Australia all have extensive plantings of fast-growing species (hardwood and softwood) and in the northern hemisphere, Scandinavian countries have all expanded their forests or controlled their use in the interests of future production.

 

详细解析: I段指出北半球的主要竞争对手为斯堪的纳维亚国家（Scandinavian countries）。


13

Which group of products is New Zealand's forestry industry now having to compete with? 

正确答案： 13 wood substitutes
Question 13

定位词: group of products, having to compete with

 

对应原文: J段, Finally, in addition to competition from other wood producers, New Zealand faces competition from goods such as wood substitutes.

 

详细解析: J段指出新西兰的林业正在与木材替代品（wood substitutes）竞争。


同义替换：group of products = goods；having to compete with = faces competition from
